name: WordPress
description: Open source CMS powering 43% of the web with extensive plugin ecosystem

description.value: Open source blog tool, publishing platform, and CMS with the largest ecosystem of themes and plugins

repository.comment: Mirror of SVN repository; development happens at github.com/WordPress/wordpress-develop

cms-type.comment: Traditional with built-in themes, but also supports headless via REST API and WPGraphQL plugin

deployment-model.comment: Self-hosted is primary; WordPress.com offers managed hosting

database-support.comment: MySQL or MariaDB required; SQLite experimental support via plugin

api-type.comment: REST API built-in since 4.7; GraphQL via WPGraphQL plugin (100k+ installs)

multi-tenancy.comment: WordPress Multisite allows multiple sites from single installation

static-site-generation.comment: Via plugins like Simply Static or headless with Gatsby/Next.js

edge-deployment.comment: PHP-based; requires traditional server, though content can be served via CDN

content-types.comment: Custom Post Types allow defining new content structures

custom-fields.comment: Native custom fields plus enhanced via ACF (Advanced Custom Fields) plugin

field-types.comment: Core fields are basic; ACF plugin adds component, repeater, and more

content-relationships.comment: Taxonomies, post relationships; enhanced via plugins like ACF or Pods

nested-components.comment: Gutenberg blocks allow nested, reusable content components

localization.comment: Via plugins like WPML, Polylang, or MultilingualPress

embedded-interactives.comment: Gutenberg blocks can contain interactive elements; shortcodes for embeds

content-versioning.comment: Built-in revision system for posts and pages

draft-publish.comment: Native draft, pending, published, and private statuses

approval-workflows.comment: Basic pending review status; full workflows require plugins like PublishPress

content-locking.comment: Post locking prevents concurrent editing conflicts

audit-logging.comment: Requires plugins like WP Activity Log; not built-in

media-library.comment: Built-in media library for images, audio, video, documents

image-transformations.comment: Auto-generates multiple sizes; basic cropping in admin

external-media-storage.comment: Via plugins like WP Offload Media for S3, Google Cloud, etc.

video-support.comment: Native video upload and embedding from YouTube, Vimeo, etc.

svg-support.comment: Disabled by default for security; requires plugin like Safe SVG

folder-organization.comment: Not built-in; requires plugins like FileBird or Real Media Library

role-based-access.comment: Built-in roles: Administrator, Editor, Author, Contributor, Subscriber

sso-saml.comment: Via plugins like miniOrange SAML or WP SAML Auth

team-collaboration.comment: WordPress 6.9 adds collaboration features with notes on blocks

audience-restrictions.comment: Private posts, password protection; membership plugins for advanced restrictions

typescript-sdk.comment: No official TypeScript SDK; community packages available

content-type-sdk.comment: register_post_type() and register_taxonomy() functions for code-first content modeling

cli-tools.comment: WP-CLI is the official command-line interface (v2.12.0)

local-development.comment: Multiple options: wp-env, Local by Flywheel, MAMP, Docker

webhooks.comment: Via plugins or custom code using WordPress hooks system

plugin-system.comment: 60,000+ plugins available; robust hook and filter system

admin-ui-customization.comment: Extensive admin customization via hooks, custom admin pages, and plugins

documentation-quality.comment: Excellent documentation with developer handbook, code reference, tutorials, and Learn WordPress

frontend-frameworks.comment: Headless WordPress integrations via REST API or WPGraphQL

search-integration.comment: Built-in search; enhanced via ElasticPress, Algolia, or Relevanssi plugins

preview-mode.comment: Built-in preview for drafts; headless preview supported via REST API

ecommerce-integration.comment: WooCommerce is the leading WordPress e-commerce plugin

analytics-integration.comment: Via plugins like Google Site Kit, MonsterInsights, etc.

ai-features.comment: Jetpack AI Assistant, various AI writing plugins available

cdn-included.comment: Self-hosted; CDN integration via plugins or hosting providers

response-caching.comment: Object caching, transients API; page caching via plugins

rate-limits.comment: Self-hosted has no limits; managed hosts may impose limits

free-tier.comment: Fully open source and free; WordPress.com has free tier with limitations

pricing-model.comment: Core software is free; paid themes, plugins, and hosting available

commercial-support.comment: Many agencies and companies offer commercial WordPress support

enterprise-features.comment: WordPress VIP offers enterprise hosting with SLA, SSO, and dedicated support
